metadata.purpose: Cultural knowledge atoms for Romance language locales

culture_refs.expression: French audiences value sophisticated, well-crafted language with literary flair
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use refined vocabulary, avoid oversimplification, intellectual rigor signals quality

culture_refs.expression: Deep connection to French heritage, art de vivre, savoir-faire
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Reference established expertise, longevity, craftsmanship over novelty

culture_refs.expression: Strong separation of religion and public life, secularism as national identity
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid religious references, focus on universal humanist values

culture_refs.expression: Pride in French uniqueness, resistance to cultural homogenization
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Position as French-adapted, not American import; highlight local relevance

culture_refs.expression: Quality of life, gastronomy, aesthetics as daily practice
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Frame products as enhancing life quality, not just productivity

taboos.reason: Perceived as vulgar and American; French prefer subtle persuasion
Overt wealth talk is considered gauche; focus on value, not price savings
Dumbing down content insults intelligence; maintain linguistic sophistication

audience_traits.notes: Titles matter (Monsieur/Madame), vous by default, build relationship before transaction

patterns.context: Soft discovery verb, not pushy; implies sophistication
Knowledge-seeking CTA aligns with intellectual values

culture_refs.expression: Strong Quebecois identity distinct from both France and English Canada
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use Quebec French vocabulary (not Parisian), acknowledge distinct identity

culture_refs.expression: Pride in maintaining French language in North American context
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Support language preservation, avoid anglicisms that feel like erasure

culture_refs.expression: Warmer, more casual than French formality; North American friendliness
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Tu form acceptable faster, casual tone works well

culture_refs.expression: Support for local Quebec businesses, buy local movement strong
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Highlight local presence, Quebec-based support

culture_refs.expression: Celebration of Quebec winters, outdoor culture, festivals
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Reference local seasons, festivals (St-Jean), outdoor lifestyle

taboos.reason: Implying Quebec French is inferior to Parisian French is deeply offensive
Using France-specific terms (portable for cellulaire, courriel not e-mail) feels foreign
French-first is law; English-dominant materials signal disrespect

audience_traits.notes: More direct than France, tu acceptable earlier, North American efficiency valued

patterns.context: Direct trial offer, North American style

culture_refs.expression: Belgian talent for finding middle ground, consensus culture
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid absolutist claims, present balanced options

culture_refs.expression: Self-deprecating humor, not taking oneself too seriously
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Lighter tone acceptable, modest claims over grandiose

culture_refs.expression: Daily navigation between French, Dutch, German; code-switching normal
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Language choice signals identity; provide French-first but acknowledge multilingual context

culture_refs.expression: Belgian comfort culture: beer, chocolate, frites, quiet prosperity
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Understated quality over flashy, reliability over innovation hype

taboos.reason: Linguistic community conflicts are politically sensitive; stay neutral
Belgians are sensitive to being treated as provincial French
Using 'septante' not 'soixante-dix' shows respect for Belgian French

audience_traits.notes: Less formal than France, more practical, appreciate efficiency with politeness

culture_refs.expression: Exactitude, punctuality, quality craftsmanship as core values
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Emphasize precision, reliability, exact specifications

culture_refs.expression: Political and ideological neutrality deeply ingrained
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid political positioning, present balanced information

culture_refs.expression: Privacy around money matters, discretion valued
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Subtle value proposition, not price-focused messaging

culture_refs.expression: Strong local canton identity, decentralized governance
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Acknowledge regional diversity, avoid Swiss generalization

taboos.reason: Financial privacy is sacrosanct; avoid money-related assumptions
Swiss deliberation valued; avoid urgency pressure tactics
Swiss French has its own character; not a French colony

audience_traits.notes: German-influenced directness, precision in communication, punctuality sacred

culture_refs.expression: Strong regional identities: Cataluña, País Vasco, Galicia, Andalucía
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid Madrid-centric view; acknowledge regional diversity

culture_refs.expression: Social life, relationships, family gatherings are central
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Frame products as enabling connection, not isolation

culture_refs.expression: Enjoying life (tapas, sobremesa, siesta culture)
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Quality of experience over efficiency metrics

culture_refs.expression: Appreciation for authentic Spanish character, traditions
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Genuine positioning over manufactured hype

taboos.reason: Catalonia/Basque independence is politically explosive
Using ustedes for plural you (instead of vosotros) sounds foreign
Historical sensitivities around dictatorship period

audience_traits.notes: Relationship-first, vosotros for plural, flexible timing expectations

patterns.context: Discovery verb, informal tú, how-it-works focus

culture_refs.expression: Strong national identity, cultural richness, pre-Hispanic heritage
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Celebrate Mexican culture, avoid U.S. condescension

culture_refs.expression: Family as cornerstone, extended family involvement
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Family benefit messaging, collective decision-making

culture_refs.expression: Mexican ingenuity ('hacer de tripas corazón'), finding solutions
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Adaptability, flexibility, problem-solving features

culture_refs.expression: Politeness rituals, indirect communication, saving face
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Warm tone, avoid confrontational language

taboos.reason: Sombrero/cactus imagery offensive; modern Mexico is sophisticated
Immigration politics extremely sensitive
Colonial references can feel patronizing

audience_traits.notes: Usted default with strangers, diminutives show warmth (ahorita, tantito)

patterns.context: Conocer (get to know) softer than discover

culture_refs.expression: Intense emotional expression, passion in everything (fútbol, tango)
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Emotional appeals work well, passion language resonates

culture_refs.expression: Argentina has most psychologists per capita; self-reflection valued
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Depth over surface, introspection-friendly messaging

culture_refs.expression: Buenos Aires identity, European heritage pride, cultural sophistication
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Sophisticated positioning, cosmopolitan references

culture_refs.expression: Street-smart cleverness, finding angles, informal workarounds
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Clever solutions, outsmarting complexity

taboos.reason: Deeply patriotic issue; never use 'Falklands' in Argentina
Repeated economic crises are painful; don't highlight financial instability
Argentines see themselves as distinct from 'rest of Latin America'

audience_traits.notes: Voseo (vos instead of tú), highly expressive, debate culture

culture_refs.expression: Exceptional friendliness, hospitality, warmth in communication
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Warm, personal tone; relationship-building language

culture_refs.expression: Strong entrepreneurial spirit, resilience, self-improvement drive
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Growth mindset messaging, business enablement

culture_refs.expression: Strong regional identities (Costeño, Paisa, Cachaco, Caleño)
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid Bogotá-only focus, acknowledge regional diversity

culture_refs.expression: Pride in Colombia's transformation narrative, moving beyond past
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Future-focused, innovation-ready positioning

taboos.reason: Narcos-style stereotypes deeply offensive; Colombia has moved on
Armed conflict is painful history, not marketing material
Colombia is highly diverse; avoid coffee/emerald only associations

audience_traits.notes: Usted common even among friends, exceptional politeness norms

culture_refs.expression: Practical, business-oriented, less ceremonial than other LATAM
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Results-focused messaging, efficiency valued

culture_refs.expression: Chile as LATAM tech hub (Start-Up Chile), innovation culture
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Innovation-friendly, early adopter positioning

culture_refs.expression: Unique Chilean modismos, distinct from Argentine/Mexican Spanish
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use Chilean expressions (cachai, po, al tiro)

culture_refs.expression: Appreciation for stability, reliability (post-2019 nuanced)
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Trust, reliability, proven solutions

taboos.reason: Still divisive; avoid political references entirely
Historical tensions with neighbors are sensitive
Chileans proud of distinct identity and progress

audience_traits.notes: Mix of tú/usted, aspirational middle class, tech-savvy

culture_refs.expression: Creative problem-solving, flexibility, finding workarounds
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Adaptable solutions, flexibility as feature

culture_refs.expression: Warmth, physical closeness, emotional expressiveness
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Personal connection emphasis, warm supportive tone

culture_refs.expression: Optimism despite challenges, 'vai dar tudo certo' mentality
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Positive framing, solution over problem

culture_refs.expression: Brazil's continental size = huge regional diversity
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid Rio/SP only; acknowledge Northeast, South differences

taboos.reason: Brazilian Portuguese is distinct language variant, not dialect
Favela/crime tourism imagery deeply offensive
Deep political division; stay completely neutral

audience_traits.notes: Você (not tu) in most regions, warmth over efficiency, personal relationship before business

culture_refs.expression: Portuguese melancholy, nostalgia for past glories, bittersweet longing
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Emotional depth resonates, heritage references work

culture_refs.expression: Portuguese resourcefulness, improvising solutions with limited means
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Efficiency, doing more with less, practical tools

culture_refs.expression: Modest self-presentation, understatement over boasting
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Subtle claims, avoid American-style hype

culture_refs.expression: Seafaring heritage, exploration history, global connections
culture_refs.marketing_angle: International reach, global connectivity

taboos.reason: Using Brazilian terms (você instead of tu) signals laziness or ignorance
Complex history; avoid both glorification and excessive criticism
Portugal is NOT Spain; distinct language and culture

audience_traits.notes: Tu conjugation, more formal than Brazil, European business norms

culture_refs.expression: Presenting oneself well, aesthetics in everything, style matters
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Beautiful design, visual excellence, elegant presentation

culture_refs.expression: Strong local city/region identity (Milan vs Rome vs Naples)
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid North-South stereotypes, acknowledge regional diversity

culture_refs.expression: Artisan quality, Made in Italy pride, craftsmanship
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Quality over quantity, attention to detail

culture_refs.expression: Family networks, personal relationships in business
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Trust-building, relationship-first approach

taboos.reason: Terroni/Polentoni slurs; regional tensions are real and sensitive
Deeply offensive stereotype, especially to Southerners
Italians can criticize Italy; foreigners cannot

audience_traits.notes: Lei form default, relationships before transactions, flexible timing

culture_refs.expression: Distinct Ticinese identity, neither Italian nor German-Swiss
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Acknowledge Ticino specificity, not Italy extension

culture_refs.expression: Swiss precision values applied to Italian warmth
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Reliability, exactitude, quality assurance

culture_refs.expression: More reserved than Italians, Swiss privacy norms
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Data privacy emphasis, discretion valued

culture_refs.expression: Alpine lifestyle, nature, outdoor values
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Work-life balance, sustainable approaches

taboos.reason: Ticinesi are Swiss, not expat Italians
Swiss reserve means softer approach needed
Swiss banking discretion culture applies

audience_traits.notes: Swiss Italian formal, Lei default, punctuality expected unlike Italy
